advantages: Directly targets overfitting by penalizing large weights, reducing variance and stabilizing predictions across retrained models.
L2 provides smooth, easy-to-optimize shrinkage compatible with gradient descent in linear models and neural nets.
L1 yields sparse models that simplify interpretation and reduce storage or inference cost by zeroing weak features.
Single hyperparameter λ is tunable with cross-validation and applies uniformly across many model classes.
disadvantages: Introduces bias: over-regularization shrinks signal along with noise, causing underfitting when λ is too large.
L1 can arbitrarily drop one of several correlated predictors; L2 retains all but may not achieve desired sparsity.
Require fair feature scaling on linear models so penalties treat each dimension equitably.
Do not replace the need for more data, better features, or architectural fixes when the model class is fundamentally misspecified.
